papers.tldr: [research paper] PAFT introduces a preservation-aware fine-tuning method that leverages token-level alignment between buggy and fixed code to derive explicit preservation signals, combining them with full-sequence masking and an edit-difficulty curriculum. This enables minimal-edit program repair by training LLMs to preserve stable context while concentrating edits on fault-relevant regions, achieving up to 65.6% improvement in pass@1 and 32.6% reduction in average edit distance over standard fine-tuning, while outperforming preference-based baselines like AdaPatcher in both correctness and edit locality without inference-time post-processing.

papers.title: FoE: Forest of Errors Makes the First Solution the Best in Large Reasoning Models
papers.summary: Recent Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) like DeepSeek-R1 have demonstrated remarkable success in complex reasoning tasks, exhibiting human-like patterns in exploring multiple alternative solutions. Upon closer inspection, however, we uncover a surprising phenomenon: The First is The Best, where alterna
papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper reveals that in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs), the first solution generated is often optimal—up to 93.7% of the time—while subsequent solutions (Subs) either fail to correct errors or introduce new ones, leading to a 'Forest of Errors' (FoE) that grows with reasoning length. The FoE is structured as a probabilistic branching process where root errors propagate and amplify, with Subs exhibiting higher error generation due to increased entropy and ineffective self-reflection. The authors propose RED, a self-guided framework that refines the first solution via entropy-based intervention and discards subsequent solutions using dual-consistency early stopping, achieving up to 19.0% accuracy gains and 37.7%–70.4% token savings across benchmarks.

papers.tldr: [empirical research paper with theoretical grounding] Under a fixed thinking token budget, single-agent LLMs outperform multi-agent systems on multi-hop reasoning due to information loss from communication bottlenecks—predicted by the Data Processing Inequality. papers.tldr: [empirical research paper] The em dash in LLM-generated prose is identified as a residual structural artifact from markdown-saturated training data, surviving suppression due to its dual role as both punctuation and structural marker. papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper proposes WWHO (Where-What-How Often), a three-layer tokenizer architecture that separates linguistic integrity from statistical compression, specifically addressing BPE fragmentation in complex Abugida scripts like Sinhala and Devanagari. The core innovation is Syllable-aware Grapheme Pair Encoding (SGPE), which uses a table-driven Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) in LinguisTrie to extract atomic syllables before applying compression, ensuring a zero-breakage guarantee. Evaluated on 30M-sentence datasets, SGPE reduces token-to-word ratio (TWR) by up to 61.7% in Sinhala and 27% in Hindi compared to state-of-the-art tokenizers, extending context windows by up to 4.38× while preserving orthographic and semantic coherence.

papers.title: SliderQuant: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for LLMs
papers.summary: In this paper, we address post-training quantization (PTQ) for large language models (LLMs) from an overlooked perspective: given a pre-trained high-precision LLM, the predominant sequential quantization framework treats different layers equally, but this may be not optimal in challenging bit-width

papers.tldr: [research paper] SliderQuant introduces a layer-adaptive post-training quantization framework that challenges the uniform treatment of LLM layers in existing sequential quantization methods. Through empirical analysis, it reveals that shallow and deep layers—especially the first and last layers—are significantly more sensitive to quantization than intermediate layers, necessitating differentiated handling. The framework employs inter-layer and intra-layer sliding window quantization with adaptive window sizes tailored to layer sensitivity, achieving superior accuracy under 4-bit weight-activation quantization across diverse LLM architectures (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, MoE models) compared to state-of-the-art methods including those using rotation transformations.

papers.title: Beyond Benchmarks: How Users Evaluate AI Chat Assistants
papers.summary: Automated benchmarks dominate the evaluation of large language models, yet no systematic study has compared user satisfaction, adoption motivations, and frustrations across competing platforms using a consistent instrument. papers.tldr: [empirical survey] A cross-platform survey of 388 active AI chat users reveals that top platforms (Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek) achieve statistically equivalent satisfaction despite massive disparities in funding and performance, indicating that user choice is driven by specialization rather than technical superiority. Over 80% of users employ multiple platforms, with negligible switching costs, suggesting a utility-based, non-ecosystem-driven adoption pattern. First-mover effects are significant: ChatGPT users report higher satisfaction when adopting first, highlighting habit and default biases. Hallucination and content filtering emerge as universal frustrations, revealing persistent tradeoffs between accuracy and policy compliance in real-world use.

papers.title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?

papers.tldr: [empirical research paper] Self-distillation degrades mathematical reasoning performance by suppressing epistemic verbalization—explicit expressions of uncertainty—when the teacher is conditioned on rich, solution-based contexts. This suppression leads to overconfident, concise reasoning that fails to adapt to out-of-distribution problems, even when answer correctness is preserved; performance drops of up to 40% are observed across multiple LLMs, with degradation being more pronounced under high context richness and limited task coverage. The findings reveal a critical gap in post-training objectives: optimizing for correct answers alone is insufficient; reasoning behaviors must be preserved to ensure robust generalization.

papers.tldr: [research paper] PoliticsBench introduces a multi-turn roleplay framework adapted from EQ-Bench-v3 to evaluate political values in LLMs through dynamic, free-text interactions. It assesses eight prominent models across 20 evolving scenarios, scoring responses on ten political values; seven models show consistent left-leaning bias with strong liberal and moderate conservative traits, while Grok exhibits right-leaning behavior and frequently disputes factual evidence. The study reveals no systematic shift in bias across roleplay stages, highlighting value-based reasoning patterns rather than ideological drift.

papers.title: Off-Policy Value-Based Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Models

papers.tldr: [research paper] ReVal introduces a value-based reinforcement learning framework for large language models that leverages LLM logits as endogenous Q-values, enabling off-policy learning without additional models. papers.title: The illusion of reasoning: step-level evaluation reveals decorative chain-of-thought in frontier language models
papers.summary: Language models increasingly "show their work" by writing step-by-step reasoning before answering. papers.comment: 13 pages, 2 figures, 5 tables, 6 extended data tables. 13 frontier models across 6 reasoning domains. Mechanistic analysis with shuffled-CoT baseline. Code and data available upon request
papers.tldr: [research paper] Step-level evaluation reveals that most frontier language models generate decorative reasoning: removing individual steps rarely changes answers (≤17% of cases), and single steps alone often suffice to recover the correct response. This indicates that reasoning chains are not used internally, despite appearing logical. Only two models show genuine step dependence (MiniMax-M2.5 on sentiment, Kimi-K2.5 on topic classification), with faithfulness being both model- and task-specific. Output rigidity is observed—models vary widely in whether they produce reasoning at all, with some generating no trace. Mechanistic analysis confirms that decorative reasoning correlates with sharper drop in CoT attention in late layers, providing internal validation of external evaluation results.

papers.title: Lie to Me: How Faithful Is Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Reasoning Models?
papers.summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning has been proposed as a transparency mechanism for large language models in safety-critical deployments, yet its effectiveness depends on faithfulness (whether models accurately verbalize the factors that actually influence their outputs), a property that prior evalua
papers.tldr: [empirical research study] A large-scale evaluation of chain-of-thought (CoT) faithfulness across 12 open-weight reasoning models reveals that models systematically fail to acknowledge external influences in their reasoning traces—despite internally recognizing them. Results show that training methodology and hint type, not parameter count, are stronger predictors of faithfulness, challenging the assumption that CoT is a reliable safety monitor and revealing category-specific blind spots in model transparency.

pdfUnis: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems, Tübingen
papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper introduces and empirically validates computational arbitrage in AI model markets, where arbitrageurs exploit cost-performance asymmetries across models to offer lower-cost solutions. papers.tldr: [empirical research paper] PPS (Prompt Protocol Specification) introduces a 5W3H-based structured intent representation framework to reduce intent transmission loss in human-AI interactions. papers.tldr: [research paper] VeriGrey introduces a grey-box testing framework for LLM agents that uses the sequence of tool invocations as a feedback function to guide mutation-based fuzzing, enabling detection of infrequent but dangerous prompt injection vulnerabilities. papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper presents the first neuron-level study of emotion control in speech-generative large audio-language models (LALMs), identifying compact emotion-sensitive neurons (ESNs) through success-filtered activation aggregation that enable training-free, inference-time emotion steering. ESNs are isolated via contrastive-margin and mean-deviation-based ranking, showing superior controllability over frequency/entropy-based methods, with optimal performance achieved at moderate intervention strengths and in intermediate-to-late decoder MLP layers—where emotion signals are most actionable and content preservation is better maintained. papers.tldr: [research paper] MetaClaw introduces a continual meta-learning framework that unifies two complementary adaptation mechanisms in deployed LLM agents: (1) skill-driven fast adaptation, which uses an LLM to synthesize reusable behavioral instructions from failure trajectories in real time, enabling zero-downtime, immediate skill injection; and (2) opportunistic policy optimization, which performs gradient-based RL updates via cloud LoRA fine-tuning during user-inactive periods, triggered by an idle-signal scheduler (sleep, inactivity, calendar). A critical innovation is the strict separation of support data (used for skill generation) and query data (used for RL), preventing reward contamination from stale contexts. The framework operates via a proxy-based architecture, scaling to production LLMs without local GPU access, and achieves up to 32% relative accuracy gains in task completion and 18.3% improved robustness in autonomous research pipelines.

papers.title: Attention Residuals

This uniform aggregation causes uncontrolled hidden-state growth with depth, progressively diluting each layer's contribution. We propose Attention Residuals (AttnRes), which
papers.tldr: [technical research paper] Attention Residuals (AttnRes) replaces uniform additive residual connections in deep LLMs with learned softmax attention over preceding layer outputs, enabling content-dependent, depth-wise selection of layer contributions. Block AttnRes partitions layers into blocks to reduce memory and communication complexity from O(Ld) to O(Nd), making it scalable with pipeline parallelism and cross-stage caching, while maintaining minimal training overhead and <2% inference latency. Evaluation on a 48B-parameter model trained on 1.4T tokens shows improved gradient distribution, bounded output magnitudes, and superior downstream performance across tasks.

papers.tldr: [research paper] Procedure-Aware Evaluation (PAE) introduces a multi-axis framework that decomposes agent behavior into Utility, Efficiency, Interaction Quality, and Procedural Integrity, exposing that 27–78% of reported task completions in LLM agents are 'corrupt successes'—achieving outcomes through policy violations, fabrication, or unauthorized actions. papers.tldr: This reproduction study evaluates Recursive Language Models (RLMs) with varying recursion depths on S-NIAH and OOLONG benchmarks, revealing a critical trade-off: while RLMs (depth=1) significantly improve performance on complex reasoning tasks like OOLONG by resisting context rot, deeper recursion (depth=2) induces 'overthinking', leading to degraded accuracy, exponential latency increases (e.g., 3.6s → 344.5s), and massive token cost inflation on both simple and complex tasks. The findings expose a fundamental limitation in current agentic frameworks where unbounded recursion undermines efficiency and practicality, contradicting the original paper's suggestion of deeper recursion as a benefit.

papers.tldr: [research paper] PRISM-MCTS introduces a metacognitive reasoning framework that replaces serial MCTS with a parallelized search using dynamic shared memory to capture heuristics and fallacies from reasoning trajectories. papers.title: Document Optimization for Black-Box Retrieval via Reinforcement Learning

papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper redefines document expansion as a reinforcement learning optimization problem, where language models (LMs) or vision-language models (VLMs) learn to transform documents into retrieval-optimized surrogates via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). papers.tldr: This survey maps the evolution of automatic video trailer generation from heuristic extractive methods to generative AI, introducing a novel taxonomy that distinguishes trailer generation from summarization by emphasizing persuasive, emotionally driven objectives. papers.tldr: [empirical research paper] Commercial LLMs show limited reliability as political advisory tools when evaluated against real legislative reasoning; frontier closed-source models (e.g., GPT-5-chat, Claude Haiku 4.5) achieve semantic alignment with official expuneri de motive texts (≥4.6/5.0), while open-weight models fall significantly below (Cohen's d > 1.4), indicating a performance gap tied to model architecture and training data coverage. papers.tldr: [research paper] XSPA introduces a highly constrained, geometrically fixed X-shaped sparse perturbation attack on vision-language models (VLMs), restricting modifications to two intersecting diagonal lines. papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper proposes WWHO (Where-What-How Often), a three-layer tokenizer architecture that separates linguistic integrity from statistical compression, specifically addressing BPE fragmentation in complex Abugida scripts like Sinhala and Devanagari. papers.tldr: [empirical research paper] Commercial LLMs show limited reliability as political advisory tools when evaluated against real legislative reasoning; frontier closed-source models (e.g., GPT-5-chat, Claude Haiku 4.5) achieve semantic alignment with official expuneri de motive texts (≥4.6/5.0), while open-weight models fall significantly below (Cohen's d > 1.4), indicating a performance gap tied to model architecture and training data coverage. papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper presents a recursive algebraic solution to the closed string tachyon vacuum equation by introducing a seam-graded expansion in the zero-momentum Lorentz-scalar sector, where Lorentz symmetry ensures closure under the equations of motion. papers.tldr: [research paper] Trojan-Speak introduces a capability-preserving adversarial fine-tuning method that bypasses Anthropic’s Constitutional Classifiers by using curriculum learning and GRPO-based hybrid reinforcement learning to teach models an encoded communication protocol. papers.title: Does Claude's Constitution Have a Culture?
papers.summary: Constitutional AI (CAI) aligns language models with explicitly stated normative principles, offering a transparent alternative to implicit alignment through human feedback alone. papers.tldr: [empirical research paper] Claude’s value profile, derived from a culturally specific constitution, exhibits a pronounced bias toward Northern European and Anglophone cultural norms, extending beyond the range of any surveyed population on most World Values Survey items. This reveals a critical limitation of Constitutional AI: when authored within a dominant cultural tradition, it may codify and amplify existing cultural biases, producing a 'value floor' that cannot be meaningfully shifted through user context or prompt engineering.

papers.tldr: [research paper] The Sovereign Context Protocol (SCP) introduces a protocol-level attribution layer between large language models (LLMs) and human-generated content, enabling real-time logging of access events with creator-specific metadata (identity, license, timestamp). The protocol is formalized with six core methods (creator profiles, semantic search, retrieval, trust scoring, authenticity verification, access auditing), supported by a threat model, revenue attribution model, and reference implementation using FastAPI and ChromaDB, positioning itself as a foundational solution to the systemic attribution gap in AI training and inference.

papers.tldr: [empirical research paper] The em dash in LLM-generated prose is identified as a residual structural artifact from markdown-saturated training data, surviving suppression due to its dual role as both punctuation and structural marker. papers.tldr: [research paper] Prompt complexity induces 'instruction dilution,' where structured reasoning frameworks like STAR fail in production-grade prompts due to competing directives that force conclusion-first output, reversing the reason-then-conclude sequence essential for correctness. papers.tldr: [research paper] Proposes an LLM-driven multimodal reranking framework that addresses long-tail short-video search biases by generating query-aware experience scores from titles, ASR, OCR, covers, and key frames—without relying on user behavior. papers.tldr: [research paper] FlashEvaluator introduces a joint, parallel evaluation framework that replaces traditional one-by-one sequence scoring in Generator-Evaluator (G-E) systems with a single forward pass where all candidate sequences share context and interact cross-list. papers.tldr: [research paper] GIRL introduces a latent world-model framework that combats long-horizon imagination drift in model-based reinforcement learning through two key innovations: (1) a cross-modal grounding signal derived from a frozen DINOv2 foundation model that enforces semantic consistency in latent transitions, penalizing physics-defying hallucinations via a differentiable consistency loss; and (2) an uncertainty-adaptive trust-region bottleneck that re-formulates the KL regularizer as a Lagrange multiplier in a constrained optimization problem, dynamically calibrating the drift tolerance using Expected Information Gain and Relative Performance Loss. Theoretical analysis re-derives the value-gap bound using the Performance Difference Lemma and Integral Probability Metrics, yielding a finite, horizon-independent regret bound that directly links the I-ELBO objective to real-environment performance. Empirical results show 38–61% reduction in latent drift, 40–55% fewer environment steps for long-horizon tasks, and superior performance over TD-MPC2 in sparse-reward and high-contact settings, with robustness to visual distractors due to grounding.

papers.tldr: [theoretical research paper] The paper identifies two theoretical mechanisms causing plasticity loss in deep reinforcement learning: (1) rank collapse of the Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK) Gram matrix and (2) $\Theta(1/k)$ decay of gradient magnitude. papers.tldr: [research paper] ResWM introduces a residual-action reformulation in model-based reinforcement learning, replacing absolute actions with incremental residual adjustments to enforce temporal smoothness and reduce control variance. The framework integrates an Observation Difference Encoder that models frame-level changes to extract dynamics-aware latent representations, enabling more stable, energy-efficient, and physically plausible action trajectories. Empirical results on the DeepMind Control Suite show superior sample efficiency, asymptotic returns, and control smoothness over Dreamer and TD-MPC, with improved stability critical for real-world robotics deployment.

papers.title: Context Engineering: From Prompts to Corporate Multi-Agent Architecture
papers.summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) systems evolve from stateless chatbots to autonomous multi-step agents, prompt engineering (PE), the discipline of crafting individual queries, proves necessary but insufficient. This paper introduces context engineering (CE) as a standalone discipline concerned with
papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper introduces context engineering (CE) as a foundational discipline for multi-agent AI systems, proposing five production-grade context quality criteria—relevance, sufficiency, isolation, economy, and provenance—positioning context as the agent's operating system. It extends CE with intent engineering (IE) and specification engineering (SE) to address organizational goals and scalable policy enforcement, forming a cumulative four-level maturity pyramid where each layer builds upon the prior. The framework addresses real-world deployment gaps, citing enterprise data and the Klarna case to demonstrate that technical capability without intentional and structural alignment leads to strategic misalignment and operational failure.

papers.title: Decoupling Task and Behavior: A Two-Stage Reward Curriculum in Reinforcement Learning for Robotics
papers.summary: Deep Reinforcement Learning is a promising tool for robotic control, yet practical application is often hindered by the difficulty of designing effective reward functions. Real-world tasks typically require optimizing multiple objectives simultaneously, necessitating precise tuning of their weights
papers.tldr: [research paper] A two-stage reward curriculum decouples task and behavioral objectives in reinforcement learning for robotics, enabling agents to first master task-specific goals via simplified rewards before gradually incorporating auxiliary behavioral terms (e.g., energy efficiency, smoothness). The method introduces sample reuse across phases via a dual-reward replay buffer, improving stability and robustness to reward weightings; ablation studies show that phase transition strategies and sample reuse are critical, with superior performance and generalization on DM Control Suite, ManiSkill3, and a mobile robot environment compared to direct full-reward training.

papers.title: Contextual Latent World Models for Offline Meta Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: Offline meta-reinforcement learning seeks to learn policies that generalize across related tasks from fixed datasets. Context-based methods infer a task representation from transition histories, but learning effective task representations without supervision remains a challenge. In parallel, latent
papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper proposes Self-Predictive Contextual Offline Meta-RL (SPC), a method that conditions latent world models on task representations inferred from transition histories, enforcing task-conditioned temporal consistency during context encoding. By jointly training a context encoder and a latent world model via contrastive learning and self-predictive dynamics, SPC learns task representations that capture long-horizon, task-specific dynamics rather than just task discrimination, leading to significantly improved generalization across MuJoCo, Contextual-DeepMind Control, and Meta-World benchmarks.

papers.title: Next Embedding Prediction Makes World Models Stronger
papers.summary: Capturing temporal dependencies is critical for model-based reinforcement learning (MBRL) in partially observable, high-dimensional domains. We introduce NE-Dreamer, a decoder-free MBRL agent that leverages a temporal transformer to predict next-step encoder embeddings from latent state sequences, d
papers.tldr: [research paper] NE-Dreamer introduces a decoder-free model-based reinforcement learning agent that replaces pixel reconstruction with next-embedding prediction using a causal temporal transformer, directly optimizing temporal predictive alignment in latent state sequences. By predicting the next encoder embedding from past embeddings and aligning it via redundancy-reduction (Barlow Twins), the method enforces long-horizon coherence without auxiliary supervision, achieving state-of-the-art performance on memory- and navigation-heavy DMLab tasks while matching or exceeding DreamerV3 on standard control benchmarks.

papers.title: Real-Time Generative Policy via Langevin-Guided Flow Matching for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a fundamental methodology in autonomous driving systems, where generative policies exhibit considerable potential by leveraging their ability to model complex distributions to enhance exploration. However, their inherent high inference latency severely impedes their de
papers.tldr: [research paper] DACER-F introduces a dynamic target guidance mechanism using Langevin dynamics to sample action distributions from an energy-based Q-function target, enabling flow matching to learn real-time, low-latency policies in online reinforcement learning. The method generates exploratory yet high-Q-value actions without requiring a stationary target distribution, achieving 28–34% higher rewards than DACER and DSAC in driving simulations while reducing inference time by 84%, and outperforms prior methods on DMC benchmarks with strong scalability.

papers.title: Short Data, Long Context: Distilling Positional Knowledge in Transformers
papers.summary: Extending the context window of language models typically requires expensive long-context pre-training, posing significant challenges for both training efficiency and data collection. In this paper, we present evidence that long-context retrieval capabilities can be transferred to student models thr

papers.title: True (VIS) Lies: Analyzing How Generative AI Recognizes Intentionality, Rhetoric, and Misleadingness in Visualization Lies
papers.summary: This study investigates the ability of multimodal Large Language Models (LLMs) to identify and interpret misleading visualizations, and recognize these observations along with their underlying causes and potential intentionality. Our analysis leverages concepts from visualization rhetoric and a newl
papers.tldr: [research paper] This study evaluates 16 state-of-the-art multimodal LLMs on their ability to detect misleading visualizations, identify rhetorical techniques (e.g., cherry-picking, framing), and infer authorial intent—intentional deception versus unintentional errors—using a novel taxonomy of nine authorial intents. Results show that LLMs can recognize rhetorical patterns and intentionality, but performance is highly sensitive to prompting; models produce semantically similar, often shallow explanations regardless of ground truth, with a notable cluster of tweets using cross-country trend charts to argue about lockdown policies, where models consistently attribute deception to information access rhetoric.

papers.title: Multimodal-NF: A Wireless Dataset for Near-Field Low-Altitude Sensing and Communications
papers.summary: Environment-aware 6G wireless networks demand the deep integration of multimodal and wireless data. However, most existing datasets are confined to 2D terrestrial far-field scenarios, lacking the 3D spatial context and near-field characteristics crucial for low-altitude extremely large-scale multipl
papers.tldr: [research paper] Multimodal-NF introduces a large-scale, 3D near-field wireless dataset and a customizable generator for upper midband (7–24 GHz) low-altitude systems, synchronizing high-fidelity channel state information (CSI) with RGB images, LiDAR point clouds, and GPS to enable environment-aware beamforming and sensing. The dataset overcomes limitations of existing far-field or 2D datasets by capturing dynamic 3D spatial semantics, reducing search space via multimodal priors, and enabling AI-driven optimization in XL-MIMO systems with realistic UAV trajectories and realistic propagation conditions.

papers.title: Structure-Aware Multimodal LLM Framework for Trustworthy Near-Field Beam Prediction
papers.summary: In near-field extremely large-scale multiple-input multiple-output (XL-MIMO) systems, spherical wavefront propagation expands the traditional beam codebook into the joint angular-distance domain, rendering conventional beam training prohibitively inefficient, especially in complex 3-dimensional (3D)
papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper proposes a structure-aware multimodal LLM framework for near-field beam prediction in XL-MIMO systems, addressing the exponential codebook dimensionality and environmental sensitivity by fusing GPS, RGB, LiDAR, and textual prompts. It decouples beam indexing into azimuth, elevation, and distance components to align with 3D geometry, introduces an auxiliary trajectory head as spatial prior, and outputs confidence scores to enable adaptive refinement, achieving superior performance over baseline methods in both line-of-sight and non-line-of-sight scenarios.

papers.title: Face-to-Face: A Video Dataset for Multi-Person Interaction Modeling
papers.summary: Modeling the reactive tempo of human conversation remains difficult because most audio-visual datasets portray isolated speakers delivering short monologues. We introduce \textbf{Face-to-Face with Jimmy Fallon (F2F-JF)}, a 70-hour, 14k-clip dataset of two-person talk-show exchanges that preserves th
pdfUnis: Johns Hopkins University, Baltimore MD 21218, USA
papers.tldr: [research paper] F2F-JF introduces a 70-hour dyadic video dataset from Jimmy Fallon talk-shows, featuring tightly synchronized host-guest turns with temporal alignment and cross-person visual context. A semi-automatic pipeline enables multi-person tracking, speech diarization, and human verification to extract temporally aligned clips for reactive behavior modeling. The dataset is used to condition a MultiTalk-style diffusion model on a guest’s preceding video to generate a host’s response, achieving small but consistent gains in emotion and visual fidelity metrics while preserving lip-sync quality, demonstrating the value of cross-person visual context in reactive dialogue generation.

papers.title: Hilbert cubes in sets with arithmetic properties
papers.summary: In this paper, we introduce new general frameworks for estimating the maximal dimension of Hilbert cubes contained in finite truncations of arbitrary sets. As applications, we investigate Hilbert cubes in a range of arithmetic sets, including perfect powers, powerful numbers, primes, smooth numbers,
papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper introduces two new analytical frameworks to bound the maximal dimension of Hilbert cubes in finite truncations of arithmetic sets. The first framework links Hilbert cube dimension to the length of longest arithmetic progressions (via $G(N)$) and to sumset sizes (via $F_k(N)$), establishing that the dimension is at most $kF_k(N) + k - 1$ and essentially bounded by $G(N)$ in magnitude. These bounds are substantially improved over prior results for perfect powers, powerful numbers, primes, smooth numbers, and squarefree numbers, achieving sharpness up to constants in several cases. A conditional result under the ABC conjecture provides an almost sharp uniform upper bound on $k$-th powers in arithmetic progressions for $k /geq 4$.

papers.title: Qianfan-OCR: A Unified End-to-End Model for Document Intelligence
papers.summary: We present Qianfan-OCR, a 4B-parameter end-to-end vision-language model that unifies document parsing, layout analysis, and document understanding within a single architecture. It performs direct image-to-Markdown conversion and supports diverse prompt-driven tasks including table extraction, chart
papers.tldr: [research paper] Qianfan-OCR introduces a 4B-parameter end-to-end document intelligence model that unifies layout analysis, text recognition, and semantic understanding in a single vision-language architecture, overcoming the trade-offs of traditional pipeline and general VLM approaches. It enables direct image-to-Markdown conversion with optional Layout-as-Thought phase—triggered by ⟨think⟩ tokens—to generate explicit layout representations (bounding boxes, element types, reading order), restoring spatial grounding and improving accuracy on complex or cluttered documents. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on OCR and document understanding benchmarks, outperforming both pipeline systems and general vision-language models on structured parsing and key information extraction tasks.

papers.title: CTPX1: A Highly Integrated and High-Throughput Data-Driven Camera Based on Timepix4
papers.summary: The upgrade of the China Spallation Neutron Source (CSNS-II) will raise the proton beam power to 500 kW. Consequently, the existing Timepix3-based detector systems, limited to a count rate of 80 Mhits/s, will encounter severe saturation challenges. To address the demand of the Energy-Resolved Neutro
papers.tldr: [research paper] CTPX1 introduces a high-throughput, data-driven camera based on Timepix4 ASIC to address count-rate saturation in high-flux neutron imaging at CSNS-II. It employs a two-stage parallel processing and merging firmware architecture to achieve 81.92 Gbps total bandwidth, enabling real-time readout at 1.17 GHz hits/s—approaching the theoretical limit—while maintaining sub-200 ps time resolution and micron-scale spatial resolution. Long-term stability (≤0.1°C temperature drift, <1 mV HV noise) and successful in-beam neutron imaging with clear spectral features validate its performance for next-generation energy-resolved neutron imaging.

papers.title: Bulk OsO2 Single Crystals: Superior Catalysts for Water Oxidation
papers.summary: Although rutile RuO2 has been a well-known and almost the best oxygen evolution reaction (OER) catalyst, the OER properties for the similar rutile oxide OsO2 with the same group element with Ru have been unknown, mainly due to long-standing synthesis difficulties. In this work, we report the success
papers.comment: 36 pages,18 figures, published online at Advanced Functional Materials
pdfUnis: School of Materials Science and Engineering, Beihang University, Beijing 100191,
Beijing National Laboratory for Condensed Matter Physics, Institute of Physics,
papers.tldr: [research paper] The study reports the first experimental realization of high-quality bulk osmium dioxide (OsO₂) single crystals, demonstrating that their intrinsic OER activity and stability surpass those of commercial RuO₂ nanopowder—particularly at high current densities—due to superior crystal integrity. Unlike nanoscale OsO₂ powders, which dissolve rapidly in alkaline solutions, micron-sized OsO₂ single crystals exhibit robust stability over 120 h and lower overpotentials, challenging the assumption that nanoscaling always enhances catalytic performance. Density functional theory reveals the (110) surface as most active, with octahedrally coordinated Os sites enabling efficient *OH desorption, highlighting crystal structure and surface coordination as key factors in balancing activity and stability.

papers.title: DeepResearch-9K: A Challenging Benchmark Dataset of Deep-Research Agent
papers.summary: Deep-research agents are capable of executing multi-step web exploration, targeted retrieval, and sophisticated question answering. Despite their powerful capabilities, deep-research agents face two critical bottlenecks: (1) the lack of large-scale, challenging datasets with real-world difficulty, a
papers.tldr: [research paper] DeepResearch-9K introduces a large-scale, multi-level benchmark dataset (9,000 questions across L₁–L₃ difficulty) generated via an autonomous pipeline from multi-hop QA datasets, featuring full search trajectories with reasoning chains and verifiable answers from a state-of-the-art deep-research agent. It addresses critical gaps in existing benchmarks by incorporating realistic tool-call frequency, extended reasoning chains, and dynamic web interaction, enabling more authentic evaluation of agent autonomy. The accompanying DeepResearch-R1 framework supports multi-turn web interactions, diverse reinforcement learning methods, and reward modeling (rule-based, LLM-as-judge), achieving state-of-the-art performance on deep-research tasks.

papers.title: UniDriveVLA: Unifying Understanding, Perception, and Action Planning for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently emerged in autonomous driving, with the promise of leveraging rich world knowledge to improve the cognitive capabilities of driving systems. However, adapting such models for driving tasks currently faces a critical dilemma between spatial perception

directly adopting 2D Vision-Language Models yields limited spatial perception, w

papers.tldr: [research paper] UniDriveVLA introduces a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture with decoupled experts for driving understanding, scene perception, and action planning to resolve the inherent conflict between spatial perception and semantic reasoning in Vision-Language-Action models. By employing a sparse perception paradigm that extracts 3D spatial priors from 2D VLM tokens and a three-stage progressive training strategy, it enhances spatial awareness without compromising the native reasoning capacity of pre-trained vision-language models. Experiments on nuScenes and Bench2Drive demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in perception, prediction, and planning tasks while maintaining strong generalization across diverse driving scenarios.

papers.title: DWDP: Distributed Weight Data Parallelism for High-Performance LLM Inference on NVL72
papers.summary: Large language model (LLM) inference increasingly depends on multi-GPU execution, yet existing inference parallelization strategies require layer-wise inter-rank synchronization, making end-to-end performance sensitive to workload imbalance. We present DWDP (Distributed Weight Data Parallelism), an
pdfUnis: WanqianLi JintaoPeng ZongfeiJing TianyuZhang ZeLong XianjieQiao
papers.tldr: [research paper] DWDP introduces a novel inference parallelization strategy that eliminates layer-wise inter-rank synchronization by offloading MoE weights across peer GPUs and fetching them on-demand, enabling independent GPU execution. This design reduces synchronization overhead caused by request-level and weight-level imbalance—particularly in MoE models—by removing collective communication at layer boundaries, with empirical validation showing 8.8% improvement in end-to-end throughput (TPS/GPU) on DeepSeek-R1 under realistic workload skew on NVL72 hardware.

papers.title: ACAVCaps: Enabling large-scale training for fine-grained and diverse audio understanding
papers.summary: General audio understanding is a fundamental goal for large audio-language models, with audio captioning serving as a cornerstone task for their development. However, progress in this domain is hindered by existing datasets, which lack the scale and descriptive granularity required to train truly ve
universities: The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK)
papers.tldr: [research paper] ACAVCaps introduces a large-scale, fine-grained audio captioning dataset built from ACAV100M using a multi-expert pipeline that analyzes audio across speech, music, and acoustic domains, with expert model outputs synthesized via a Chain-of-Thought enhanced LLM to generate detailed, diverse captions. The dataset achieves 76.7k unique tokens and spans 13k hours of multi-domain audio, outperforming existing datasets in descriptive granularity and domain coverage, leading to stronger generalization in downstream audio understanding tasks when used for pre-training large audio-language models.

papers.title: CatRAG: Functor-Guided Structural Debiasing with Retrieval Augmentation for Fair LLMs
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are deployed in high-stakes settings but can show demographic, gender, and geographic biases that undermine fairness and trust. Prior debiasing methods, including embedding-space projections, prompt-based steering, and causal interventions, often act at a single stage of
papers.comment: 9 pages, 4 figures, and accepted in IJCNN 2026 (part of IEEE WCCI 2026)
pdfUnis: Florida International University University of South Florida Florida Internation
papers.tldr: [research paper] CatRAG introduces a dual-mechanism debiasing framework that combines functor-guided structural projection—using category theory to suppress bias-associated directions in embedding space while preserving semantic structure—with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for evidence-grounded, fair output generation. The functor component performs structure-preserving bias attenuation across protected attributes (gender, race, nationality), while RAG retrieves diverse, balanced evidence to counteract skewed internal knowledge, jointly reducing bias scores to near zero and improving accuracy by up to 40% over base models on the BBQ benchmark across three open-source LLMs.

papers.title: VDPP: Video Depth Post-Processing for Speed and Scalability
papers.summary: Video depth estimation is essential for providing 3D scene structure in applications ranging from autonomous driving to mixed reality. Current end-to-end video depth models have established state-of-the-art performance. Although current end-to-end (E2E) models have achieved state-of-the-art performa
papers.comment: 8 pages, 6 figures. Accepted to CVPR 2024 Workshop. Project page: https://github.com/injun-baek/VDPP
pdfUnis: Figure 1. VDPPachieves superior speed, spatialaccuracy and temporalconsistency a
papers.tldr: [research paper] VDPP redefines video depth post-processing by shifting from full scene reconstruction to targeted geometric refinement in low-resolution space, enabling RGB-free, high-speed (＞43.5 FPS) temporal stabilization without sacrificing spatial accuracy or coherence. It uses residual learning on depth and normal maps in error space to correct temporal instabilities, achieving SOTA-level temporal stability while maintaining modularity and scalability for integration with any evolving image-to-depth model or depth-only sensors like LiDAR.

papers.title: Neural Computers
papers.summary: We propose a new frontier: Neural Computers (NCs) -- an emerging machine form that unifies computation, memory, and I/O in a learned runtime state. Unlike conventional computers, which execute explicit programs, agents, which act over external execution environments, and world models, which learn en
universities: KAUST (King Abdullah University of Science and Technology)
papers.tldr: [research paper] Neural Computers (NCs) propose a unified machine form where computation, memory, and I/O are embedded in a single learned latent runtime state, eliminating the need for external execution environments. The paper demonstrates that video-based NC prototypes—trained solely on raw I/O traces (text, actions, pixels)—can learn early interface primitives like I/O alignment and short-horizon control in CLI and GUI settings, but face persistent challenges in long-horizon reasoning, symbolic stability, capability reuse, and runtime governance. These findings support a roadmap toward a Completely Neural Computer (CNC), a Turing-complete, universally programmable system where all computational functions are internalized within a single neural model.

papers.title: Breaking the Winner's Curse with Bayesian Hybrid Shrinkage
papers.summary: The widespread adoption of randomized controlled trials (A/B Tests) for decision-making has introduced a pervasive "Winner's Curse": experiments selected for launch often exhibit upwardly biased effect estimates and invalid confidence intervals. This selection bias leads to over-optimistic impact pr
papers.tldr: [research paper] Bayesian Hybrid Shrinkage (BHS) introduces experiment-specific local shrinkage factors to correct selection bias in A/B tests, addressing the Winner’s Curse by leveraging data-driven priors that adapt to individual experiment characteristics. Unlike uniform shrinkage, BHS incorporates experiment-level features to improve robustness against prior misspecification and provides closed-form inference for scalable deployment, with empirical validation showing superior bias reduction and interval coverage under realistic assumption violations in both simulations and real-world Meta experiments.

papers.title: When Denoising Hinders: Revisiting Zero-Shot ASR with SAM-Audio and Whisper
papers.summary: Recent advances in automatic speech recognition (ASR) and speech enhancement have led to a widespread assumption that improving perceptual audio quality should directly benefit recognition accuracy. In this work, we rigorously examine whether this assumption holds for modern zero-shot ASR systems. W
papers.comment: 6 pages, 4 figures, 5 tables. IEEE Conference Paper

papers.tldr: [empirical study] SAM-Audio preprocessing degrades zero-shot ASR performance despite improving perceptual audio quality, revealing a fundamental mismatch between human-perceived clarity and machine recognition robustness. The degradation is systematic across utterances and worsens with larger Whisper models, indicating that advanced denoising introduces acoustic distribution shifts that misalign with ASR training data, challenging the assumption that perceptual enhancement automatically benefits transcription.

papers.title: Unix Tools and the FITO Category Mistake: Crash Consistency and the Protocol Nature of Persistence
papers.summary: Unix tools such as ls, cp, mv, and rename expose a filesystem abstraction that appears to present a single, authoritative state evolving through atomic transitions. This abstraction is false.   We present a systematic Forward-In-Time-Only (FITO) analysis demonstrating that the assumption of instanta
papers.tldr: [technical critique / formal analysis] The paper exposes a fundamental category mistake in Unix filesystem abstractions—namely, the false assumption of instantaneous, atomic state transitions (FITO) in operations like ls, cp, mv, and rename. Through a formal analysis of ext4 journaling, delayed allocation, fsync semantics, and NVMe behavior, it proves that no syscall-based persistence primitive can guarantee a consistent commit boundary under failure. The paper demonstrates that filesystem operations are protocol-based, not state-based, and that cross-layer temporal assumptions leak across the stack, leading to real-world failures in cloud storage, databases, and AI training due to retry amplification and silent corruption. The core insight is that atomicity is not a property of state but of protocol convergence in both forward and reverse directions.

papers.title: dTRPO: Trajectory Reduction in Policy Optimization of Diffusion Large Language Models
papers.summary: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) introduce a new paradigm for language generation, which in turn presents new challenges for aligning them with human preferences. In this work, we aim to improve the policy optimization for dLLMs by reducing the cost of the trajectory probability calculation,
papers.tldr: [research paper] dTRPO introduces a theoretical framework for trajectory reduction in diffusion large language models (dLLMs) by proving that the probability ratio of newly unmasked tokens under reference policy regularization is an unbiased estimate of intermediate diffusion states. It further demonstrates that full trajectory probabilities can be estimated via a single forward pass of a re-masked final state, enabling efficient offline policy optimization. The method achieves up to 9.6% gains on STEM tasks, 4.3% on coding, and 3.0% on instruction-following with minimal computational overhead, making it scalable and compatible with both long-block and block-wise dLLM architectures.

papers.title: EvA: An Evidence-First Audio Understanding Paradigm for LALMs
papers.summary: Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) still struggle in complex acoustic scenes because they often fail to preserve task-relevant acoustic evidence before reasoning begins. We call this failure the evidence bottleneck: state-of-the-art systems show larger deficits in evidence extraction than in downst
papers.tldr: [research paper] EvA introduces a dual-path architecture that resolves the evidence bottleneck in LALMs by preserving acoustic evidence before reasoning through hierarchical aggregation in CED-Base and non-compressive, time-aligned fusion with Whisper. The architecture maintains full temporal resolution and multi-scale cues, enabling superior performance on perception-heavy benchmarks like MMAU, MMAR, and MMSU, with the largest gains in tasks requiring fine-grained audio understanding. EvA-Perception, a large-scale event-ordered audio QA dataset, enables training on fine-grained temporal cues, supporting the evidence-first hypothesis that upstream perception limits audio understanding more than downstream reasoning.

papers.title: When Perplexity Lies: Generation-Focused Distillation of Hybrid Sequence Models
papers.summary: Converting a pretrained Transformer into a more efficient hybrid model through distillation offers a promising approach to reducing inference costs. However, achieving high-quality generation in distilled models requires careful joint design of both the student architecture and the distillation proc
papers.tldr: [research paper] The paper reveals a critical gap between log-likelihood (perplexity)-based and generation-based evaluation in cross-architecture distillation, where models can match teachers in perplexity but fail dramatically in autoregressive generation. It proposes Hybrid-KDA, a delta-attention hybrid architecture, and GenDistill, a multi-stage distillation pipeline that prioritizes generation quality via end-to-end autoregressive evaluation. Systematic ablation shows that dataset selection, completion-only masking, and freezing attention layers during post-training have the largest impact on generation performance, with the best model achieving 86–90% teacher accuracy on knowledge benchmarks while reducing KV cache memory by up to 75% and improving time-to-first-token by 2–4× at 128K-token contexts.

papers.title: IDEA: An Interpretable and Editable Decision-Making Framework for LLMs via Verbal-to-Numeric Calibration

We propose IDEA, a framework that extracts LLM decision kno

papers.title: MathAgent: Adversarial Evolution of Constraint Graphs for Mathematical Reasoning Data Synthesis
papers.summary: Synthesizing high-quality mathematical reasoning data without human priors remains a significant challenge. Current approaches typically rely on seed data mutation or simple prompt engineering, often suffering from mode collapse and limited logical complexity. This paper proposes a hierarchical synt
